mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, which compresses profit margins and triggers short-term negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of periods where INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the consumer price index below 0.1% confirms disinflationary momentum, allowing equity valuations to expand and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of weeks following CPIAUCSL MoM declines below 0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate, where payrolls rise but unemployment simultaneously increases, signals labor market slack and hidden weakness that forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day returns are positive in > 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, which compounds over the quarter to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands alongside real sales of final goods during a manufacturing upcycle, synchronized supply and demand strength boosts operating leverage and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 65% of months where INDPRO rises above 0.5% during manufacturing upcycles.
